source.title: Explosion and Fire on Liftboat Brazos at Vermilion Block 170 A

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Vermilion Block 170 A

actions_taken: Non-essential workers reported to muster area
Fire extinguished using fire water hose and wheeled fire extinguishers
First aid provided to injured workers in galley
USCG contacted for evacuation of two injured workers
Both injured workers evacuated by USCG helicopter and transferred to medical facility
Onsite incident investigation conducted by BSEE
Preservation order issued for flame arrestor, 500-barrel tank, and generator
Generator relocated to rental company facility for thorough inspection
event.summary: On August 11, 2024, at approximately 7:10 p.m., an explosion and fires occurred on the deck of the liftboat Brazos at Vermilion Block 170 A during a well cleanup and test operation. Two Production Technology & Services Inc. (PTS) workers sustained second-degree burns and required evacuation by USCG. The incident resulted from improperly latched hatches on a 500-barrel tank allowing gas to escape, which accumulated near a rental diesel generator. The generator's faulty electronic control module failed to shut down the engine when it overran due to gas ingestion, causing ignition of the gas plume. Contributing factors included lack of written procedures, inadequate hazard analysis, poor maintenance of tank seals, and failure of the generator's automatic emergency shutdown system.
recommendations: BSEE Lake Charles District recommends the Office of Incident Investigations issue a Safety Alert/Bulletin regarding this incident

threats.name: Unsealed tank hatches allowing gas escape
Gas accumulation in work area due to calm wind conditions
Generator air intake drawing in accumulated gas
Generator engine overspeed due to gas ingestion
Failure of automatic air intake shutdown valve

supporting_text: The three 24-inch hatches on the 500-barrel tank were not properly latched

BSEE investigators observed that the first and third hatch latch-bolts were all loose. The second hatch had one latch-bolt that was not secured to the hatch, and the other four latch-bolts were also loose
The O-rings on the hatches were not maintained, as they were absent of grease, showed visible cracks, and signs of deterioration

controls.name: Tank O-ring maintenance and inspection
controls.barrier_role: Maintain seal integrity of tank hatches
controls.lod_basis: Preventive maintenance to ensure primary barrier function

supporting_text: The O-rings were found absent of fresh grease. The O-rings were then removed from the groove and found to have visible cracks and signs of deterioration. Corrosion was also observed on the seating face of the hatch cover

The PTS Manager explained that the tank is equipped with an atmospheric vent and a pressure safety valve set at 20 psi. The three 24-inch hatch O-rings are inspected to ensure they are in good condition, then greased and reinstalled into the hatch covers

controls.name: Generator automatic air intake shutdown device
controls.barrier_role: Prevent engine overspeed and ignition of gas
controls.lod_basis: Secondary control to prevent ignition source activation

supporting_text: The generator's faulty electronic control module prevented the air intake shut-off valve from automatically shutting down the engine when it reached its over-frequency setpoint
The control module displayed alarm codes for Emergency Stop, Air Flap Closed, and Gen Over frequency Shutdown in the event log

When they tried to activate the manual electronic emergency stop push button on the exterior of the enclosure the air intake valve failed to close. The technicians then attempted to shut the air intake valve electronically, simulating an automatic emergency shutdown (ESD), but were unsuccessful
The electronic control module was removed, and upon inspection, the circuit board showed signs of deterioration and delamination

controls.name: Generator pre-rental testing and documentation
controls.barrier_role: Verify generator safety systems function before deployment
controls.lod_basis: Quality assurance for equipment safety systems

supporting_text: The technician explained that the generator was serviced, cleaned, and all settings in the control module were verified. It was also mentioned that the ESD was tested before dispatch. The BSEE investigators requested copies of testing documentation, but the technician stated that the tests were not documented
the technician admitted that the automatic electronic ESD could not be tested without a technician, however the manual electronic emergency stop button could be tested

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) and hazard identification
controls.barrier_role: Identify and communicate hazards before work begins

supporting_text: There was an inadequate hazard analysis conducted for this situation. None of the JSAs mentioned the potential for a large amount of gas to be released during the emptying of fluids from the separator

On the morning of August 11, 2024, at approximately 5:30 a.m., Gulf held a day shift pre-tour safety meeting with PTS, which included a job safety analysis (JSA) and a sequence of planned events for the day

controls.name: Written procedures for equipment commissioning and decommissioning
controls.barrier_role: Standardize and document safe work practices

supporting_text: There were no written procedures for commissioning or decommissioning the temporary well cleanup and test equipment
When BSEE investigators asked about written procedures for draining fluids out of the separator after the well test, the PTS Day Supervisor admitted there were none in place

BSEE investigators asked the PTS Manager if there were written procedures for prepping the 500-barrel tank or written procedures for draining fluids from the separator after the well test is completed, the PTS Manager responded that there are no written procedures

controls.name: Personal gas detection equipment
controls.barrier_role: Detect hazardous gas presence and alert workers

supporting_text: All PTS workers were equipped with personal gas detectors provided by PTS. Upon inspection, BSEE investigators found that the detectors were Honeywell BW Clip H2S detectors designed specifically for detecting the presence of H2S gas only

The rate at which gas was displaced through the unsealed hatches, combined with calm wind conditions, allowed a gas plume to accumulate near the 500-barrel tank and generator, creating a hazardous atmosphere on the work deck

controls.name: Flame arrestor on tank atmospheric vent

supporting_text: BSEE investigators noticed an atmospheric vent with a flame arrestor above the 500-barrel tank, and no visual fire damage was observed. The flame arrestor was approximately ten feet above the tank
The flame arrestor had a dent to the exterior housing; however, the element showed no fire damage and appeared clean and free of obstructions
A pressure safety valve with a set point of 20 psi was visually inspected and showed no signs of fire damage
The tank is equipped with an atmospheric vent and a pressure safety valve set at 20 psi
The general alarm sounded on the liftboat, and non-essential workers reported to the muster area

Workers from Laredo, PTS, and Gulf responded to the fire, which was extinguished in approximately 15 minutes using at least one fire water hose from the liftboat and four wheeled fire extinguishers on the work deck
BSEE investigators observed two 33-gallon foam-wheeled fire extinguishers discharged as well as two 300-pound purple-K wheeled fire extinguishers discharged on the work deck

controls.name: Emergency medical response and evacuation
controls.barrier_role: Provide emergency medical care and evacuation

supporting_text: Two PTS workers sustained second degree burns to their face, neck, and hands requiring medical attention. They received first aid in the galley of the liftboat while waiting for evacuation
At 7:23 p.m., the Gulf representative contacted their field helicopter for an evacuation of the two injured workers

At 8:16 p.m., the Laredo Liftboat Captain contacted the USCG requesting an evacuation of both injured workers. The USCG arrived at 10:51 p.m. and hoisted both injured workers into the helicopter, transferring them to a medical facility in Lafayette by 12:14 a.m. on August 12, 2024

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
